steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes how Chinese immigrants from the north (Mandarin speakers) settled in Queens, while those from the south (Cantonese/Fuzhounese speakers) settled in Manhattan — mirroring the regional language geography of China. The question asks what can be inferred from this pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — language change rates: The passage says nothing about how quickly languages change. ✗
  • Choice B — regional language patterns from an origin country can replicate in a destination country: This is exactly what the passage shows — the north/south linguistic split from China is reproduced in New York. ✓
  • Choice C — linguistic variation within a language: The passage discusses different languages (Mandarin vs. Cantonese), not variation within a single language. ✗
  • Choice D — country size correlates with number of languages: Country size is never mentioned. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
